info.name: KPN-HCIS Enterprise API
info.description:
Comprehensive API test collection for KPN-HCIS Backend.

Phases covered:
  Phase 1 — Auth (JWT access + refresh token pair)
  Phase 2 — RBAC (CASL PermissionsGuard + CheckPermissions)
  Phase 3 — Organization Units (Adjacency List + recursive CTE tree)
  Phase 4 — Leave Request Lifecycle (XState v5 state machine)
  Phase 5 — AI Analytics (Qwen/DashScope BI layer)

Workflow: Run "Login" first — the test script auto-populates {{jwtToken}} and {{refreshToken}} for all subsequent requests.

item.name: 🔑 Authentication
item.description: Public login and token management endpoints. Run Login first — the test script saves accessToken → {{jwtToken}} and refreshToken → {{refreshToken}} automatically.

exec: pm.test("Response has accessToken — saved to {{jwtToken}}", function () {
pm.test("Response has refreshToken — saved to {{refreshToken}}", function () {

request.description: Authenticate with email and password. Returns accessToken (15 min), refreshToken (7 days), and expiresIn (seconds). The test script auto-saves both tokens to the active environment.

Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access + refresh token pair. Send the refresh token in the Authorization: Bearer header (pre-configured via {{refreshToken}}). Returns 200 OK with a new AuthTokensDto.
Invalidates the current session by clearing the stored refreshTokenHash on the user record. Returns 204 No Content. Subsequent refresh attempts will fail with 401.

item.name: 👤 Users Management
item.description: Full CRUD for user accounts. All routes require JWT. Permissions enforced by CASL PermissionsGuard: create/read/update/delete on subject 'User'.

exec: pm.test("Response contains id — saved to {{userId}}", function () {
pm.test("Sensitive fields are not exposed", function () {

request.description: Creates a new user account. employeeId must be unique. roleIds is an array of Role UUIDs. unitId (optional) links the user to an organizational unit. Password is hashed server-side — never returned.

query.description: Full-text search on fullName and email

request.description: Paginated user list. Returns { data: UserResponseDto[], total: number }. Enable optional query params to filter by unit or full-text search.

variable.description: User UUID — auto-populated by Create User test script

request.description: Returns a single UserResponseDto. Throws 404 if the user does not exist or has been soft-deleted.
Partial update — only send fields you want to change. All fields are optional. Changing password requires passing the new password string; it will be re-hashed.
Soft-delete: sets deletedAt timestamp. The user record is retained for audit trail and referential integrity. Returns 204 No Content.

item.name: 🏢 Organization Units
item.description: Organizational unit hierarchy management. Uses PostgreSQL recursive CTEs for tree traversal and ancestor chain resolution. All routes require 'read Unit' permission minimum.

query.description: Set true to include soft-deactivated units

request.description: Returns the full org chart as a nested UnitTreeNode tree built via an O(n) in-memory Map from a single DB query. Each node contains: id, name, code, description, parentId, isActive, createdAt, updatedAt, children[].
Flat alphabetical list of all active units. Suitable for dropdowns and search-ahead selects in the frontend.

exec: pm.test("Response contains unit id — saved to {{testUnitId}}", function () {
pm.test("Unit code matches uppercase pattern", function () {

request.description: Create a new unit. code must be uppercase alphanumeric with underscores/hyphens only (regex: ^[A-Z0-9_-]+$). Optionally add parentId (UUID) to nest under an existing unit. Throws 409 if code is already taken.

variable.description: Unit UUID — auto-populated by Create Unit test script

request.description: Single unit with parent summary (id, name, code) and _count of children and users. Throws 404 if not found.

Returns the full ancestor chain from the org root down to (and including) this unit, ordered shallowest-first. Uses a PostgreSQL recursive CTE. The frontend uses this array to render breadcrumb navigation.
Partial update. All fields are optional. Changing parentId triggers a circular-hierarchy validation — setting a descendant as the new parent returns 400 Bad Request.
Soft-deactivate: sets isActive = false. Does NOT cascade to children — callers must deactivate the subtree explicitly if needed. Returns 200 OK with the updated unit record.

item.name: 📝 Leave Management (XState Engine)
item.description:
Full leave request lifecycle powered by XState v5. State machine: DRAFT → PENDING_APPROVAL → APPROVED_BY_SUPERVISOR → APPROVED / REJECTED / CANCELLED.

Workflow: Create (DRAFT) → Submit → Approve (supervisor) → Approve (HRD final) or Reject/Cancel at any non-terminal state.

exec: pm.test("Response contains id — saved to {{leaveRequestId}}", function () {
pm.test("XState context is seeded in payload", function () {

request.description: Creates a new leave request in DRAFT state for the authenticated user. leaveType enum: ANNUAL | SICK | MATERNITY | PATERNITY | SPECIAL | UNPAID. totalDays min: 0.5, max: 365 (step 0.5). The XState context snapshot is seeded into the payload JSONB column.

query.description: ISO 8601 date — filter startDate >= this value
ISO 8601 date — filter startDate <= this value
Approvers only — query another user's requests

request.description: Paginated list with optional filters. Regular employees always see only their own requests (userId is overridden to request.user.id). Approvers/HRD can pass ?userId= to query other employees within their CASL-scoped unit.

variable.description: Leave Request UUID — auto-populated by Create test script

request.description: Returns the full leave request with all LeaveApproval audit rows included (ordered by decidedAt ASC). Throws 404 if not found.

Returns { status: LeaveRequestStatus, allowedEvents: string[] } for the current XState state node. The Vue 3 / Quasar frontend uses this response to conditionally render action buttons — only reachable transitions are shown. Example: { status: 'PENDING_APPROVAL', allowedEvents: ['APPROVE_BY_SUPERVISOR', 'REJECT', 'CANCEL'] }
XState event: SUBMIT. Transitions DRAFT → PENDING_APPROVAL. Optional approverIds (UUID array) pre-designates which approvers are authorized. Returns 200 OK with the updated leave request.

Smart approval — auto-routes to the correct XState event based on current status:
  PENDING_APPROVAL       → APPROVE_BY_SUPERVISOR (supervisor step)
  APPROVED_BY_SUPERVISOR → APPROVE (HRD final step)
  Any other status       → 409 ConflictException

The authenticated user's ID is implicitly used as approverId — approvers never pass their own ID, preventing impersonation. Returns 200 OK.

XState event: REJECT. Transitions PENDING_APPROVAL or APPROVED_BY_SUPERVISOR → REJECTED. remarks is required (validated by RejectLeaveRequestDto). The reason is persisted in both the LeaveApproval audit row and payload.transitions[]. Returns 200 OK.

XState event: CANCEL. Valid from DRAFT, PENDING_APPROVAL, and APPROVED_BY_SUPERVISOR. Terminal states (APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED) return 409 ConflictException. No request body required. Returns 200 OK with the cancelled leave request.

item.name: 🤖 AI Analytics (BI Layer)
item.description: Qwen AI (DashScope / Alibaba Cloud) powered analytics. Aggregates leave and attendance data per organizational unit and returns structured BI reports. Requires QWEN_API_KEY and QWEN_BASE_URL to be set in the server environment.

request.description: Aggregates leave data for the specified unit, constructs a structured prompt, and sends it to the Qwen model (configured via QWEN_BASE_URL + QWEN_API_KEY). Returns a BI report with risk indicators, trend analysis, and actionable HR recommendations. Response time depends on QWEN_TIMEOUT_MS (default: 30 000 ms).

pm.test("Response time is under 2000 ms", function () {

pm.test("Payload personal block is present", function () {
pm.test("Payload employment block has department_id", function () {
pm.test("Payload manager_snapshot block is present", function () {
pm.test("passwordHash is NOT exposed in the response", function () {
pm.test("employeeId matches the sent employee_no — save to env", function () {
// Run this request twice with the same body — both should return 200.
// Check the Console tab: synced_at timestamps will differ,
// confirming the upsert ran (not a cache hit).
console.log("🔄 Last synced_at:", json.payload && json.payload.darwinbox && json.payload.darwinbox.synced_at);

request.description:
Upserts one Darwinbox employee into the KPNHCIS users table.

HTTP 200 for both create (first sync) and update (re-sync) — upsert semantics.

Required: coreDetails.employee_no (upsert key) + coreDetails.email.
Optional: profileDetails (biographical/address), employmentDetails (position/history).

Permissions: sync:User — integration service account only.

Side effects:
  • unitId resolved via Unit.code = department_id (null if unit not created yet)
  • managerId resolved via User.employeeId = reporting_manager_employee_id (null if manager not yet synced)
  • New users get a temp unusable passwordHash; must use password-reset flow to log in
  • payload.positionIds (PLT dual-position UUIDs) are preserved — never overwritten by sync

console.log("📊 Department query returned", json.total, "total users,", json.data.length, "on this page");

request.description:
Lists employees belonging to a Darwinbox department_id.

Uses GIN @> containment query on payload.employment.department_id — fast even at high user counts.

{{syncedDepartmentId}} is set automatically by the 'Sync Employee Profile' Tests script.

Permissions: read:User.

Query params:
  skip — offset (default 0)
  take — page size (default 20)
